title: Ceph cluster OSD nearfull and backfillfull capacity pressure
dataSources: `ceph osd df` / status JSON indexed as `ceph:osd` with `utilization`, `apply_class_*` fields
app: Custom scripted input or community Ceph TA; Universal Forwarder + HEC
spl:
index=storage sourcetype="ceph:osd" earliest=-30m
| eval util=coalesce(utilization, pcent, space_used_percent)
| where util > 85 OR state="nearfull" OR state="backfillfull" OR match(_raw, "nearfull|backfillfull")
| stats max(util) as max_util values(state) as states by osd_id, cluster_name
| sort - max_util
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` max(Performance.storage_used_percent) as used_pct
  from datamodel=Performance where nodename=Performance.Storage
  by Performance.host Performance.object span=1h
| where used_pct > 80
| sort - used_pct
dataModelAcceleration: Enable Data Model Acceleration for the CIM Performance data model (Performance.Storage node / storage_used_percent) after vendor fields are aliased to CIM; otherwise the cimSpl tstats variant may return no rows.
description: OSDs approaching nearfull or backfillfull slow rebalancing and risk write failures during recovery. Per-OSD utilization highlights imbalanced CRUSH weights or failed additions.
value: Prevents cluster-wide ingest stalls and gives operators time to expand before PGs cannot backfill.
implementation: Run `ceph osd df -f json` every 5 minutes via scripted input; flatten `nodes` array into one event per OSD. Set `props.conf` for `ceph:osd`. Page when util > 85% for any OSD class serving production pools.
visualization: Bar chart (util by OSD), table (osd_id, util, host).
knownFalsePositives: Backfill and rebalancing after node changes can move the cluster toward full before new capacity is absorbed; check recovery and CRUSH activity in the cluster manager.
grandmaExplanation: We warn when Ceph disks are getting too full so the cluster can rebalance in time and you avoid write stops or emergency expansion.
